name: get_search_console_performance
description: Query the connected Search Console property's Search Analytics: clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position by query/page/country/device/date. First-party data — use it for what already ranks, near-ranking queries, and pages with real demand. ctr is a 0-1 fraction; position is a 1-based average; dates are Pacific Time; the last ~3 days may be incomplete. Read-only and free (no credits).

projectId.description: Required. The OpenSEO project ID to scope this call to. Get one from list_projects.

dimensions.description: Group rows by these dimensions. Default ['query']. Use ['page'] for top pages, ['query','page'] to map queries to pages / spot cannibalization, ['date'] for a time series.

dateRange.description: Convenience window (default last_28_days). End is set ~3 days back for GSC data lag. Ignored if startDate+endDate are given. Max lookback is 16 months.

startDate.description: Explicit start (YYYY-MM-DD, Pacific Time). Use with endDate.

endDate.description: Explicit end (YYYY-MM-DD, Pacific Time). Use with startDate.

filters.description: AND-combined filters. To get the queries for one page: [{dimension:'page',operator:'equals',expression:'https://example.com/post'}] with dimensions ['query'].

rowLimit.description: Rows per call (default 1000, max 1000). GSC sorts by clicks desc and can't filter by position — filter 'striking distance' positions client-side, and paginate with startRow when hasMore is true.
